info.title: Course Scheduler API
info.description: HTTP API for generating course schedules using constraint satisfaction solving

post.description:
Submit a new schedule generation request.

**Usage:**
```python
httpx.post("http://localhost:8000/submit", json=body)
```

get.description:
Get details about a schedule session.

**Usage:**
```python
httpx.get(f"http://localhost:8000/schedules/{sid}/details")
```

post.description:
Generate all remaining schedules for a session asynchronously.

**Usage:**
```python
httpx.post(f"http://localhost:8000/schedules/{sid}/generate_all")
```

get.description:
Get the current count of generated schedules for a session.

**Usage:**
```python
httpx.get(f"http://localhost:8000/schedules/{sid}/count")
```

Get a previously generated schedule by index.

**Usage:**
```python
httpx.get(f"http://localhost:8000/schedules/{sid}/index/0")
```

post.description:
Immediate cleanup of a schedule session.

**Usage:**
```python
httpx.post(f"http://localhost:8000/schedules/{sid}/cleanup")
```

ClassPattern-Input.title: ClassPattern
ClassPattern-Input.description:
Represents a class pattern.

**Usage:**
```python
ClassPattern(credits=3, meetings=[...])
```

ClassPattern-Output.title: ClassPattern
ClassPattern-Output.description:
Represents a class pattern.

**Usage:**
```python
ClassPattern(credits=3, meetings=[...])
```

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of schedules to generate

CombinedConfig.title: CombinedConfig
CombinedConfig.description:
Represents a combined configuration.

**Usage:**
```python
CombinedConfig(config=..., time_slot_config=..., limit=10)
```

conflicts.title: Conflicts
conflicts.description: List of course IDs that cannot be scheduled simultaneously

CourseConfig-Input.title: CourseConfig
CourseConfig-Input.description:
Represents a course configuration.

**Usage:**
```python
CourseConfig(course_id="CS 101", credits=3, room=[...], lab=[...], conflicts=[], faculty=[])
```

conflicts.title: Conflicts
conflicts.description: List of course IDs that cannot be scheduled simultaneously

CourseConfig-Output.title: CourseConfig
CourseConfig-Output.description:
Represents a course configuration.

**Usage:**
```python
CourseConfig(course_id="CS 101", credits=3, room=[...], lab=[...], conflicts=[], faculty=[])
```

lab_index.title: Lab Index
lab_index.description: When `lab` is set, index into `times` for the lab meeting.

CourseInstanceResponse.title: CourseInstanceResponse
CourseInstanceResponse.description: One course row in a generated schedule (`CourseInstance.model_dump` JSON shape).

maximum_credits.title: Maximum Credits
maximum_credits.description: Maximum credit hours they can teach

maximum_days.title: Maximum Days
maximum_days.description: Maximum number of days they are willing to teach (0-5, optional)

minimum_credits.title: Minimum Credits
minimum_credits.description: Minimum credit hours they must teach

unique_course_limit.title: Unique Course Limit
unique_course_limit.description: Maximum number of different courses they can teach

times.title: Times
times.description: Dictionary mapping day names to time ranges

course_preferences.title: Course Preferences
course_preferences.description: Dictionary mapping course IDs to preference scores

room_preferences.title: Room Preferences
room_preferences.description: Dictionary mapping room IDs to preference scores

lab_preferences.title: Lab Preferences
lab_preferences.description: Dictionary mapping lab IDs to preference scores

mandatory_days.title: Mandatory Days
mandatory_days.description: Set of days the faculty must teach on

FacultyConfig-Input.title: FacultyConfig
FacultyConfig-Input.description:
Represents a faculty configuration.

**Usage:**
```python
FacultyConfig(name="Dr. Smith", maximum_credits=12, minimum_credits=3, ...)
```

maximum_credits.title: Maximum Credits
maximum_credits.description: Maximum credit hours they can teach

maximum_days.title: Maximum Days
maximum_days.description: Maximum number of days they are willing to teach (0-5, optional)

minimum_credits.title: Minimum Credits
minimum_credits.description: Minimum credit hours they must teach

unique_course_limit.title: Unique Course Limit
unique_course_limit.description: Maximum number of different courses they can teach

times.description: Dictionary mapping day names to time ranges

course_preferences.description: Dictionary mapping course IDs to preference scores

mandatory_days.description: Set of days the faculty must teach on

FacultyConfig-Output.title: FacultyConfig
FacultyConfig-Output.description:
Represents a faculty configuration.

**Usage:**
```python
FacultyConfig(name="Dr. Smith", maximum_credits=12, minimum_credits=3, ...)
```

GenerateAllResponse.title: GenerateAllResponse
GenerateAllResponse.description:
Response model for generate-all schedule requests.

**Usage:**
```python
GenerateAllResponse(message='...', current_count=1, target_count=10)
```

**Fields:**
- message: Status message about the generation process
- current_count: Number of schedules already generated
- target_count: Target number of schedules to generate

HealthCheck.title: HealthCheck
HealthCheck.description:
Health check response model.

**Usage:**
```python
HealthCheck(status="healthy", active_sessions=0)
```

**Fields:**
- status: Health status of the service
- active_sessions: Number of active schedule generation sessions

duration.title: Duration
duration.description: Duration of the meeting in minutes

lab.title: Lab
lab.description: Whether the meeting is in a lab

Meeting.title: Meeting
Meeting.description:
Represents a single meeting instance.

**Usage:**
```python
Meeting(day="MON", duration=90, lab=False)
```

MessageResponse.title: MessageResponse
MessageResponse.description:
Generic message response model.

**Usage:**
```python
MessageResponse(message="ok")
```

**Fields:**
- message: Response message text

ScheduleCountResponse.title: ScheduleCountResponse
ScheduleCountResponse.description:
Response model for schedule count requests.

**Usage:**
```python
ScheduleCountResponse(schedule_id='...', current_count=2, limit=10, is_complete=False)
```

**Fields:**
- schedule_id: Unique identifier for the schedule session
- current_count: Number of schedules currently generated
- limit: Maximum number of schedules to generate
- is_complete: Whether all schedules have been generated

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of schedules to generate

ScheduleDetailsResponse.title: ScheduleDetailsResponse
ScheduleDetailsResponse.description:
Response model for schedule details requests.

Inherits all fields from CombinedConfig and adds:

**Usage:**
```python
ScheduleDetailsResponse(schedule_id='...', total_generated=0, **combined.model_dump())
```

**Fields:**
- schedule_id: Unique identifier for the schedule session
- total_generated: Total number of schedules generated

ScheduleResponse.title: ScheduleResponse
ScheduleResponse.description:
Response model for schedule retrieval requests.

**Usage:**
```python
ScheduleResponse(schedule_id='...', schedule=[...], index=0, total_generated=1)
```

**Fields:**
- schedule_id: Unique identifier for the schedule session
- schedule: Generated schedule as `list[CourseInstanceResponse]` (typed JSON rows)
- index: Index of this schedule in the generation sequence
- total_generated: Total number of schedules generated so far

SchedulerConfig-Input.title: SchedulerConfig
SchedulerConfig-Input.description:
Represents a scheduler configuration.

**Usage:**
```python
SchedulerConfig(rooms=[...], labs=[...], courses=[...], faculty=[...])
```

SchedulerConfig-Output.title: SchedulerConfig
SchedulerConfig-Output.description:
Represents a scheduler configuration.

**Usage:**
```python
SchedulerConfig(rooms=[...], labs=[...], courses=[...], faculty=[...])
```

SubmitResponse.title: SubmitResponse
SubmitResponse.description:
Response model for schedule submission requests.

**Usage:**
```python
SubmitResponse(schedule_id="...", endpoint="/schedules/...")
```

**Fields:**
- schedule_id: Unique identifier for the generated schedule session
- endpoint: URL endpoint to access the schedule

start.description: Start time of the time block

spacing.title: Spacing
spacing.description: Time spacing between slots in minutes

end.description: End time of the time block

TimeBlock-Input.title: TimeBlock
TimeBlock-Input.description:
Represents a time block within a day.

**Usage:**
```python
TimeBlock(start="09:00", spacing=60, end="17:00")
```

start.description: Start time of the time block

spacing.title: Spacing
spacing.description: Time spacing between slots in minutes

end.description: End time of the time block

TimeBlock-Output.title: TimeBlock
TimeBlock-Output.description:
Represents a time block within a day.

**Usage:**
```python
TimeBlock(start="09:00", spacing=60, end="17:00")
```

start.title: Start
start.description: Start time in minutes since midnight.

TimeInstanceResponse.title: TimeInstanceResponse
TimeInstanceResponse.description: One meeting time block within a scheduled course (JSON shape).

start.description: Start time of the time range

end.description: End time of the time range

TimeRange-Input.title: TimeRange
TimeRange-Input.description:
A time range with start and end times, ensuring start < end.

**Usage:**
```python
TimeRange(start="09:00", end="17:00")
```

start.description: Start time of the time range

end.description: End time of the time range

TimeRange-Output.title: TimeRange
TimeRange-Output.description:
A time range with start and end times, ensuring start < end.

**Usage:**
```python
TimeRange(start="09:00", end="17:00")
```

times.title: Times
times.description: Dictionary mapping day names to time blocks

max_time_gap.title: Max Time Gap
max_time_gap.description: Maximum time gap between time slots to determine if they are adjacent

TimeSlotConfig-Input.title: TimeSlotConfig
TimeSlotConfig-Input.description:
Represents a time slot configuration.

**Usage:**
```python
TimeSlotConfig(times={...}, classes=[...])
```

times.title: Times
times.description: Dictionary mapping day names to time blocks

max_time_gap.title: Max Time Gap
max_time_gap.description: Maximum time gap between time slots to determine if they are adjacent

**Usage:**
